top_signals.title: 'Both Sides Are Lying': Fereidun Fesharaki on Oil Flows
top_signals.summary: FGE NexantECA Chairman Emeritus Fereidun Fesharaki says "both sides are lying" as the conflict between the US and Iran continues, adding that oil prices are currently driven by sentiment rather than physical movements. He speaks on Insight With Haslinda Amin. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Keppel ramps up Hanoi property push as housing prices soar
Chile copper mines pursue tie-up to cut costs
top_signals.summary: Sierra Gorda and Spence will assess joint opportunities as both operations tackle lower grades and productivity pressures.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: What should we make of Makerfield?
top_signals.summary: Much analysis of the Makerfield by-election focuses on what it means for Keir Starmer and the Labour party’s national fortunes – but how should we assess it on its own terms? Asks Steve Akehurst It can be stated without much hyperbole that when the people of Makerfield go to the polls next week it will [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
